methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 22.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Black Semiconductor is a Series A chip design company founded by Daniel and Sebastian Schall that has raised €254.4M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 4 same-stage semiconductor companies: Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), Substrate ($1.0B), and Rain AI ($500M), with valuations spanning $500M to $5.0B.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach given the absence of disclosed revenue. The median valuation across the 4 Series A comparables is $1.5B, anchoring the central estimate. Public semiconductor multiples (P/S median of 22.3x across ALAB, NVDA, and AMD) cannot be applied without target revenue disclosure, which is typical for private companies at this stage.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.3B to $1.7B, centered at $1.5B. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion in peer valuations—a 10x spread from Rain AI to Etched—which reflects heterogeneity in technology differentiation and funding environments within the Series A cohort. The relevance of each comparable to Black Semiconductor's specific technical approach and market position materially affects the weight assigned within this range.
